channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What would it mean for Ukraine if it actually starts producing Patriot missiles?
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Fire breaks out at a pub in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people, officials say
item.description: Footage shared online by first responders shows a huge blaze raging and plumes coming out of the front door of the pub in the northern part of the Thai capital.
item.content:encoded:
Footage shared online by first responders shows a huge blaze raging and plumes coming out of the front door of the pub in the northern part of the Thai capital.

(Image credit: Sakchai Lalit)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Toronto police looking for suspects after deadly shooting at festival
item.description: At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.
item.content:encoded:
At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.

(Image credit: Keito Newman)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal helps lift Argentina past Switzerland 3-1 and into World Cup semifinals
item.description: Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.
item.content:encoded:
Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.

(Image credit: Abbie Parr)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. launches new strikes on Iran and Tehran attacks U.S. allies in the Gulf
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire for the third weekend in a row, with strikes stretching into Monday as Iran responded to U.S. attacks by targeting U.S. allies in the region.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire for the third weekend in a row, with strikes stretching into Monday as Iran responded to U.S. attacks by targeting U.S. allies in the region.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Palestinian aid worker who organized World Cup screenings killed in Israeli strike
item.description: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
item.content:encoded: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: A veteran foreign correspondent looks back on a career covering conflicts
item.description: For Reporter's Notebook we hear about what it takes to cover conflict over a decades-long career as a foreign correspondent.
item.content:encoded: For Reporter's Notebook we hear about what it takes to cover conflict over a decades-long career as a foreign correspondent.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Most people who need glasses don't have them. Can the post office change that?
item.description: In some towns in India, a visitor to the post office who's squinting at fine print might be asked: Do you want an eye test?
item.content:encoded:
In some towns in India, a visitor to the post office who's squinting at fine print might be asked: Do you want an eye test?

(Image credit: Subhamoy Bhattacharjee for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Spain edges Belgium and will face France in World Cup semifinal
item.description: Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.

(Image credit: Mark J. Terrill)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump threatens Iran after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral saw calls for his killing
item.description: Trump made the comments on his Truth Social after U.S. officials demanded that Iran make a public statement saying the Strait of Hormuz is open.
item.content:encoded:
Trump made the comments on his Truth Social after U.S. officials demanded that Iran make a public statement saying the Strait of Hormuz is open.

(Image credit: Iranian Supreme Leader Office)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
